project.name: FORGE
project.description: A PoW-backed AI agent compute token formed through HACD Stack. Every FRG token was formed on-chain through a PoW-mined HACD unit and a 50 HAC stack cost. 1 FRG = 1 prepaid AI agent task run. Tokens burn on use. New supply only through HACD Stack minting.
project.target_users: AI developers, protocol users, HACD Stack participants seeking verifiable compute credits
project.why_hacd: Compute credits that can be freely minted are structurally indistinguishable from promises. FORGE anchors supply origin to real energy expenditure — every FRG batch has an immutable on-chain formation record that cannot be backdated or secretly pre-minted.

launch_phases.label: Public Stack
launch_phases.description: All 200 HACD lots open to public participation via Hacash Launchpad. Each lot: 1 HACD + 50 HAC stack cost → 10,000 FRG formed on-chain.

utility.holder_rights: Each FRG token represents one prepaid AI agent task run. The token is consumed (burned) when the task executes.
utility.product_utility: 1 FRG = 1 AI agent task. Burn-on-use model. No passive income, no staking rewards, no guaranteed redemption value.
utility.ecosystem_utility: FRG tokens are the native spend unit for FORGE AI agent tasks built on the HACD Stack. Demand is driven by actual task execution.
utility.utility_notes: Task execution infrastructure is in active development. This token represents a formation record and future task access claim — not a currently active compute service.

launch.public_communication_angle: PoW-backed compute credits formed through HACD Stack — every FRG has a verifiable on-chain formation origin

demo.formation_status: mocked for demo — no real on-chain transactions
